findings.problem: The change-local capability specs do not fully encode the shared spawn-governance contract for index refresh. They require `agent-spawn-decision-v1` before spawning a fresh verifier, but they do not normatively require the same decision record before spawning `index-maintainer`, even though the shared workflow contracts and the change's own design/tasks treat that rule as mandatory for any new verifier or index-maintainer session.
findings.evidence: `openspec/changes/introduce-index-aware-verifier-workflow/specs/index-aware-verification-preflight/spec.md` requires stale or missing coverage to invoke `index-maintainer`, but it does not require writing or validating an `agent-spawn-decision-v1` record before that spawn. `openspec/changes/introduce-index-aware-verifier-workflow/specs/ai-enforced-workflow/spec.md` explicitly requires `agent-spawn-decision-v1` only for fresh verifier spawns. In contrast, `openspec/schemas/ai-enforced-workflow/verification-sequence.md`, `openspec/schemas/ai-enforced-workflow/index-sequence.md`, `/home/madejuele/.codex/skills/openspec-index-preflight/SKILL.md`, and `openspec/changes/introduce-index-aware-verifier-workflow/tasks.md` all require a validated spawn-decision record before any new verifier or index-maintainer session. The frozen artifact set therefore still has a specs-to-design/tasks/shared-sequence mismatch on a checkpoint-gating rule.
findings.recommendation: Add a normative requirement and scenario in the change-local specs that any `index-maintainer` spawn for refresh is preceded by an `agent-spawn-decision-v1` record validated against `openspec/schemas/ai-enforced-workflow/agent-spawn-decision-v1.schema.json`, and align the preflight and ai-enforced-workflow capability specs with the shared sequence wording that no new verifier or index-maintainer session may be opened ad hoc.
